title: Inventory satellite impervious-surface, drainage, rainfall, flood-extent, and population data sources for South Asian megacities
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates satellite impervious-surface products (Landsat), SAR flood-extent maps (Sentinel-1), rainfall data (CHIRPS, GPM), drainage-infrastructure data, and population-exposure data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit drainage-data availability assessment.
inputs: Sentinel-1 SAR flood-mapping methodology and urban accuracy studies
CHIRPS and GPM rainfall product documentation
OpenStreetMap drainage coverage assessment for target cities
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, resolution, and documented limitations — particularly SAR accuracy in urban canyons and drainage-data availability.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering resolution, urban accuracy, and drainage-data availability.

title: Map impervious-surface fraction and change-over-time at neighborhood scale for at least two South Asian megacities
outcome: A neighborhood-scale impervious-surface analysis for at least two South Asian megacities, documenting Landsat classification methodology, accuracy assessment, and change-over-time trends.
inputs: Landsat time-series data for target cities
Administrative boundary data for neighborhood aggregation
expected_artifact: validation.md update with impervious-surface methodology and accuracy assessment.
validation_method: Replicator confirms Landsat classification methodology, accuracy assessment against reference data, and change-over-time documentation for each target city.
done_condition: Impervious-surface analysis includes methodology, accuracy metrics, change-over-time trends, and replication status for at least two cities.

title: Estimate drainage-capacity insufficiency using available drainage data and impervious-surface proxies
outcome: A drainage-capacity gap analysis combining available drainage-infrastructure data with impervious-surface proxies and rainfall-extreme data, with explicit documentation of drainage-data limitations and gap-filling assumptions.
inputs: OpenStreetMap or municipal drainage data where available
Published drainage-capacity studies for target cities
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with drainage methodology and data-limitation documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms drainage-data integration methodology, gap-filling assumptions, and data-limitation documentation for each target city.
done_condition: Drainage-capacity analysis includes methodology, data sources, gap-filling assumptions, data-limitation documentation, and replication status.

title: Produce neighborhood-scale pluvial flood-risk map combining impervious surface, drainage, and rainfall data
outcome: A neighborhood-scale flood-risk map for at least two South Asian megacities, with explicit validation against historical flood-extent data and documentation of informal-settlement exposure.
inputs: Sentinel-1 SAR historical flood-extent data for validation
WorldPop population and informal-settlement boundary data
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with risk methodology and historical-validation results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms risk-integration methodology, historical-flood validation, and informal-settlement exposure documentation for each target city.
done_condition: Flood-risk map includes methodology, historical-validation results, informal-settlement exposure documentation, and replication status for at least two cities.

title: Red-team flood-risk map misuse for eviction and displacement of informal settlements
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how flood-risk maps could be used to justify eviction of informal-settlement residents without providing alternative housing or drainage improvement, and how drainage-data limitations could create false precision in risk classification.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every flood-risk claim has drainage-data limitation documentation, informal-settlement exposure context, and explicit eviction-risk disclaimers. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every flood-risk claim is either blocked, softened to data-limited language with explicit eviction-risk disclaimers, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match municipal drainage and urban-planning decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the flood-risk analysis would change any real decision for municipal engineers, urban planners, or drainage authorities without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for municipal and urban-planning audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that flood maps are used for eviction rather than drainage investment, or that drainage-data limitations create false precision. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Mumbai Municipal Corporation drainage engineer), named decision (e.g., drainage-upgrade investment targeting), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for informal-settlement eviction without alternative housing), and replicated field-reality review.
